title: impatiens capensis f. platymeris
intro: Impatiens capensis f. platymeris, also known as orange jewelweed or orange touch-me-not, is a perennial herb belonging to the balsam family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be effective in treating skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in salicylic acid, a compound that has been shown to have pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as eczema, acne, and insect bites. Herbal preparations of Impatiens capensis f. platymeris, including infusions and salves, can be used topically to soothe and protect the skin.
